provenance.title: Empirical Asset Pricing via Machine Learning
affiliations: Booth School of Business, University of Chicago
Yale University, AQR Capital Management, and NBER
Booth School of Business, University of Chicago
provenance.abstract: We perform a comparative analysis of machine learning methods for the canonical problem of empirical asset pricing: measuring asset risk premiums. We demonstrate large economic gains to investors using machine learning forecasts, in some cases doubling the performance of leading regression-based strategies from the literature.
key_claims: Machine learning forecasts yield large economic gains — NN-based S&P 500 timing achieves annualized Sharpe ratio of 0.77 vs. 0.51 buy-and-hold
Long-short decile portfolio sorted on NN forecasts earns annualized Sharpe ratio of 1.35 (value-weighted), 2.45 (equal-weighted)
Trees and neural networks are best-performing methods, tracing gains to nonlinear predictor interactions
All methods agree on the same dominant predictive signals: momentum, liquidity, and volatility
Shallow learning (NN3) outperforms deep learning (NN4, NN5) due to small financial data sets and low signal-to-noise ratio

architecture.primary_variant: Feed-forward neural network with ReLU activations (NN1–NN5 variants)

models_compared.name: OLS
models_compared.description: Pooled ordinary least squares with all 920 predictors

models_compared.name: OLS-3
models_compared.description: OLS restricted to size, book-to-market, momentum only

models_compared.name: PCR
models_compared.description: Principal components regression — reduce P predictors to K components via SVD then OLS

models_compared.name: PLS
models_compared.description: Partial least squares — dimension reduction maximizing covariance with forecast target via SIMPLS

models_compared.name: GLM
models_compared.description: Generalized linear model with spline basis expansion and group lasso penalty

models_compared.name: GBRT
models_compared.description: Gradient boosted regression trees — ensemble of shallow trees fitted to residuals with shrinkage

models_compared.name: RF
models_compared.description: Random forest — bootstrap aggregation of deep trees with random feature subsets

neural_network_detail.hidden_layers: 1-5 layers, neuron counts follow geometric pyramid rule (Masters 1993)

modules.name: PredictorConstruction
modules.role: Build zi,t feature vector from characteristics × macro interactions
modules.formula: zi,t = xt ⊗ ci,t concatenated with industry dummies

modules.name: ReturnPredictor
modules.role: Model g(zi,t) = Et(ri,t+1) — conditional expected excess return

modules.name: HyperparameterTuner
modules.role: Select tuning parameters via validation sample

modules.name: PortfolioConstructor
modules.role: Build bottom-up portfolio forecasts and decile-sorted long-short portfolios

mathematical_spec.name: Return prediction model
mathematical_spec.role: Overarching additive prediction-error model. g(·) is estimated by each ML method.

mathematical_spec.name: Feature vector construction
mathematical_spec.latex: z_{i,t} = x_t \otimes c_{i,t}, \quad P = P_c \times P_x = 94 \times (8+1) + 74 = 920
mathematical_spec.role: Kronecker interaction of stock characteristics with macro predictors plus industry dummies

mathematical_spec.name: OLS objective (L2 loss)
mathematical_spec.role: Standard pooled OLS training objective

mathematical_spec.name: Weighted least squares objective
mathematical_spec.role: Value-weighted or time-equal-weighted variant of OLS

mathematical_spec.name: Huber robust loss
mathematical_spec.role: Robust training objective for heavy-tailed financial returns; applied to OLS, ENet, GLM, GBRT

mathematical_spec.name: Elastic net penalty
mathematical_spec.role: L1+L2 regularization; rho=0 → lasso (sparsity), rho=1 → ridge (shrinkage)

mathematical_spec.name: Group lasso penalty (GLM)
mathematical_spec.role: Select or zero out all K spline basis terms for each characteristic as a group

mathematical_spec.name: GLM spline basis expansion
mathematical_spec.role: Nonlinear univariate spline transformations of each predictor (no interactions)

mathematical_spec.name: Tree prediction
mathematical_spec.role: Piecewise-constant prediction over K rectangular partitions of depth L

mathematical_spec.name: Tree impurity (L2)
mathematical_spec.role: Branch splitting criterion: minimize L2 impurity at each node

mathematical_spec.name: PCR dimension reduction objective
mathematical_spec.role: Find K components that best preserve predictor variance (via SVD of Z)

mathematical_spec.name: PLS dimension reduction objective
mathematical_spec.latex: w_j = \arg\max_w \mathrm{Cov}^2(R, Zw), \quad \text{s.t.} \; w^\top w = 1, \; \mathrm{Cov}(Zw, Zw_l) = 0 \; \forall l < j
mathematical_spec.role: Find K components maximizing covariance with return target (solved via SIMPLS)

mathematical_spec.name: Neural network recursive formula
mathematical_spec.role: Forward pass through hidden layers with ReLU activation; linear output layer

mathematical_spec.name: Out-of-sample R-squared
mathematical_spec.role: Primary predictive performance metric; benchmark is zero forecast (NOT historical mean)

mathematical_spec.name: Diebold-Mariano test statistic
mathematical_spec.role: Pairwise model comparison test; positive DM → column model outperforms row model

mathematical_spec.name: Campbell-Thompson Sharpe ratio improvement
mathematical_spec.role: Translate predictive R² into Sharpe ratio improvement for a market-timing investor

mathematical_spec.name: Variable importance (R² reduction)
mathematical_spec.role: Importance of predictor j = drop in R² when all values of predictor j are set to zero

mathematical_spec.name: Variable importance (SSD)
mathematical_spec.role: Sensitivity of model output to each input — sum of squared partial derivatives over training set
mathematical_spec.note: Not applicable to tree models — mean decrease in impurity used instead

mathematical_spec.name: Portfolio return forecast (bottom-up)
mathematical_spec.role: Aggregate stock-level forecasts into portfolio-level forecasts using known ex-ante weights

mathematical_spec.name: Maximum drawdown
mathematical_spec.role: Risk measure for long-short decile portfolios

mathematical_spec.name: Portfolio turnover
mathematical_spec.role: Average monthly portfolio rebalancing cost proxy

tensor_semantics.name: R
tensor_semantics.description: Panel of all excess stock returns ri,t+1; NT = total stock-month observations

tensor_semantics.name: Z
tensor_semantics.description: Full predictor matrix; rows = stock-month observations, columns = 920 features

tensor_semantics.name: c_it
tensor_semantics.description: Cross-sectionally ranked stock characteristics (mapped to [-1,1]), one row per stock per month

tensor_semantics.name: x_t
tensor_semantics.description: 8 macro predictors + constant; common to all stocks at time t

tensor_semantics.name: z_it
tensor_semantics.description: Feature vector for stock i at time t: Kronecker product of x_t and c_it plus 74 industry dummies

tensor_semantics.name: x_l (NN hidden layer output)
tensor_semantics.description: Output of hidden layer l in NN; K(l) neurons. NN3: l=1→32, l=2→16, l=3→8

tensor_semantics.name: r_hat_it
tensor_semantics.description: Predicted excess return for stock i at time t from model g(z_it; theta)

tensor_semantics.name: Omega_K
tensor_semantics.description: Dimension reduction weight matrix (PCR: principal components; PLS: SIMPLS components)

optimizer.name: Adam (for NN); Accelerated proximal gradient (for ENet, GLM); Greedy recursive splitting (for trees)
optimizer.learning_rate: Adaptive via Kingma & Ba (2014) Adam with learning rate shrinkage
optimizer.notes: Exact Adam hyperparameters not stated; standard defaults assumed

lr_schedule.type: Learning rate shrinkage (Algorithm 5 in Internet Appendix B.3)
lr_schedule.decay: Adaptive shrinkage toward zero as gradient approaches zero

batch_size.note: SGD with random mini-batches; exact batch size not stated in paper

training_splits.training: 1957–1974 (18 years, grows 1 year at each refit)
training_splits.test: 1987–2016 (30 years, never used for estimation or tuning)

regularization_techniques: Early stopping (terminate when validation loss starts rising)
Batch normalization (cross-sectional demean and variance-standardize each hidden unit per batch)
Ensemble over multiple random seeds (average predictions to reduce variance)

ensemble.description: Multiple random seed initializations; average forecasts across seeds
ensemble.note: Exact number of ensemble seeds not stated

data_preprocessing.cross_sectional_rank: All 94 characteristics cross-sectionally ranked period-by-period and mapped to [-1, 1]
data_preprocessing.missing_values: Replace with cross-sectional median at each month
data_preprocessing.publication_lag: Monthly chars: 1 month lag; quarterly: 4 month lag; annual: 6 month lag
data_preprocessing.macro_predictors: From Welch and Goyal (2008): dp, ep, bm, ntis, tbl, tms, dfy, svar

datasets.name: CRSP Monthly Stock Returns
datasets.description: All NYSE, AMEX, NASDAQ common stocks; monthly total returns; 1957-03 to 2016-12

datasets.name: Stock Characteristics
datasets.description: 94 firm-level predictive characteristics from Green, Hand, Zhang (2017)

datasets.name: Macro Predictors
datasets.description: 8 macroeconomic predictors from Welch and Goyal (2008): dp, ep, bm, ntis, tbl, tms, dfy, svar

datasets.name: Industry Dummies
datasets.description: 74 industry sector dummies based on first two SIC digits

metrics.name: R²_oos (monthly, panel)
metrics.description: Out-of-sample R² benchmarked against zero forecast (NOT historical mean)

metrics.name: R²_oos (annual, panel)
metrics.description: Annual horizon predictive R²; roughly 10× larger than monthly

metrics.name: Diebold-Mariano test statistic
metrics.description: Pairwise model comparison; N(0,1) under null; Bonferroni-adjusted for 12-way comparisons

metrics.name: Annualized Sharpe ratio
metrics.description: For long-short decile and market-timing portfolios

metrics.name: Monthly portfolio turnover
metrics.description: Average fraction of portfolio rebalanced per month

portfolios_evaluated: Fama-French SMB, HML, RMW, CMA, UMD

compute.description: Research Computing Center at University of Chicago
compute.note: Specific hardware details not provided in paper

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Adam optimizer with lr=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, eps=1e-8 for neural network training
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard Adam defaults (Kingma & Ba 2014); paper specifies Adam with learning rate shrinkage but not exact values

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Mini-batch size of 512 stock-month observations per SGD step
implementation_assumptions.basis: Common practice for tabular financial data; paper uses SGD but does not state batch size

implementation_assumptions.assumption: 10 ensemble random seeds for neural network prediction averaging
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper describes ensemble but does not state number of seeds; 10 is common practice

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Early stopping patience of 5 validation epochs before terminating
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard early stopping heuristic; paper describes the procedure but not the patience parameter

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Batch normalization applied after each hidden layer activation
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard placement per Ioffe & Szegedy (2015); paper states batch normalization is used

implementation_assumptions.assumption: L1 regularization lambda for NN tuned via validation grid search over {0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1}
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard grid for L1; paper states L1 penalization used but not the grid

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Spline order K=3 (cubic splines) as default for GLM basis expansion
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard spline choice; paper specifies order-2 spline series form but K is a tuned hyperparameter

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Random forest: 300 bootstrap trees, m = sqrt(P) features per split
implementation_assumptions.basis: Breiman (2001) default recommendations; paper tunes B and m via validation

implementation_assumptions.assumption: GBRT: 1000 trees, shrinkage nu=0.01, tree depth L=1 (stumps) or L=2
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper reports trees have 'on average less than 6 leaves' and uses shallow trees

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Framework: PyTorch for neural networks; scikit-learn for trees; NumPy/SciPy for linear methods
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard Python ML stack for academic finance; paper does not specify framework

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Cross-sectional rank normalization maps characteristics to [-1, 1] via rank / (N_t/2) - 1
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper states 'cross-sectionally rank all stock characteristics and map to [-1, 1]' following Kelly, Pruitt, Su (2019)

ambiguities.description: Exact mini-batch size for SGD not stated
Number of random seeds used for neural network ensemble averaging not stated
Early stopping patience / number of epochs not stated in main paper
Specific knot locations c1..c_{K-2} for spline basis not specified
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Equally spaced quantiles of training data

ambiguities.description: Exact risk-free rate proxy source (Treasury bill rate) — Welch-Goyal data implied but not confirmed as source of rf
ambiguities.primary_assumption: 1-month T-bill rate from Ibbotson via CRSP
